title: Healthy Habits: Foot Hygiene
license_notes: Use as public patient-education source support. Avoid verbatim copying into primitives.
supports: Tinea pedis, often called athlete's foot or ringworm, is a fungal infection of the skin and feet.
It often affects the spaces between the toes.
Keeping feet and toes clean and dry and changing shoes and socks regularly can help prevent or control tinea pedis.
Warm, dark, moist environments increase fungal growth.
